author: nflaig
content: <@412614104222531604> <@808969530608451584> need to make sure that the tx have highest priority fee and ideally also get sent to the mempool at the same time to be included in a single payload, although you can sent much more than 8192 to make sure we can fill a block, also this requires batching to work with 193M gas

author: nflaig
content: do you have an error log, I suspect it's failing the ssz serialization?

author: pk910
content:
lighthouse throws:
```
May 05 17:43:17.600 WARN  Execution engine call failed                  error: BadResponse("Failed to convert json to execution requests: DecodeError(\"Failed to decode DepositRequest from EL: BytesInvalid(\\\"VariableList of 12300 items exceeds maximum of 8192\\\")\")")
May 05 17:43:17.600 WARN  Error processing HTTP API request             elapsed_ms: 298.338394, status: 400 Bad Request, path: /eth/v3/validator/blocks/40, method: GET
```

geth doesn't show a explicit error,  but that'S kinda expected, no?
I think el clients must keep track of the number of deposits when building blocks, so they skip deposits when the limit is reached

author: nflaig
content: yes, this breaks liveness, same error will happen with any CL client and the EL is completely unaware and keeps building these invalid payloads, we need to fix this one way or the other, either increase CL limit further or have the EL limit the amount of deposit txs they pack per payload. This is not very likely to happen in practice but a wealthy attacker (eg. nation state) could abuse this. Also worth pointing out we should not rely on builders "to safe us" here because an attacker could also trigger the circuit breaker at the same time.
